title: Reef Science Talks
description: In-depth reef ecology and coral biology content. Bleaching, disease, spawning, fish communities, and the science behind reef resilience.

title: Community Monitoring Program
description: Everything volunteers and new participants need for our Kāneʻohe Bay Community Reef Monitoring Program. Methods, safety, scheduling, and data literacy.

title: GIS Cert Tutorials
description: QGIS and R tutorials for marine biologists and reef managers. Spatial analysis, transect mapping, and data visualization for field scientists.

title: Ocean Sounds / Focus
description: Ambient ocean recordings, slack-key instrumental sessions, and focus-friendly audio. For studying, writing, or just decompressing after a long field day.

title: Slack-Key Sessions
description: Saved slack-key guitar live sessions and studio performances. Hawaiian fingerstyle tradition — for listening and unwinding.

title: Data and Reporting
description: Tutorials and guides on interpreting reef monitoring data, writing science communication reports, and using CoralNet and related tools.

title: Ocean Conservation Policy
description: Videos on MPAs, ocean policy, invasive species management, and the intersection of reef science and governance in Hawaii and the Pacific.

title: Quick Tips and Shorts
description: Reef science and monitoring tips in under a minute.

title: Free-Diving and Scuba Basics
description: Safety, gear, and technique for recreational divers and community reef monitors doing shallow surveys. Breath-hold safety and scuba orientation.

title: Species Identification Guides
description: Field identification for coral genera, invasive algae, and reef fish. Visual references for volunteers and amateur marine naturalists.
